title: Psychotria micralabastra
intro: Psychotria micralabastra, also known as the "Wild Mountain Mint," is a tropical plant species native to the rainforests of Central and South America, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The health benefits of Psychotria micralabastra include its traditional use in treating fever, colds, and respiratory issues due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Psychotria micralabastra is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and is also used to make tinctures and capsules, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.

The Rubiaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants that includes many species of gardenias, coffee, and gardenias.
